title: auth — helper functions
description: Three global helpers. Everything else exposed by auth is behind DI / facades — helpers here cover the very-hot request-scoped lookups downstream modules would otherwise repeat in every file.

helpers.purpose: Return the authenticated principal regardless of guard. Delegates to `auth()->user()` under `sanctum` or `platform_admin`. Returns null on unauthenticated requests OR ServiceAccount principals (those go through the JWT auth.jwt_verify middleware, which populates auth.jwt_claims instead of setting a user).
behaviours: Returns User instance on sanctum-authenticated requests
Returns PlatformUser instance on platform_admin-authenticated requests
Returns null on unauthenticated / service-account-authenticated requests
Cached in the request-scoped container so repeated calls in one request don't repeat guard resolution

helpers.purpose: Return the resolved scope of the current session (application_id, tenant_id, roles, permissions) without a repeat DB round-trip. Reads from the auth:session_scope:token:{token_id} cache; falls back to the Sanctum PAT's abilities compact array on cache miss.
behaviours: Returns SessionScope on any authenticated request

helpers.purpose: Convenience wrapper around JwtSigner::sign() for the common case (Application-scoped, per-session JWT). Handles kid resolution + `app` claim binding + jti generation. NEVER used for interactive sessions (those use Sanctum PAT) — reserved for inter-service call paths where a downstream module needs to propagate the caller's context.
behaviours: Resolves the active JwtSigningKey for the subject's Application
Sets iat=now(), exp=now()+auth.jwt.ttl_seconds (or extended per entitlement)
Binds `app` claim to the subject's application_id
Signs with HS256 via the KMS-decrypted secret (stack-scoped)

notes: Helpers are registered from `src/Support/helpers.php`. Namespaced functions are safe to call across every framework layer including migrations + seeders (though the auth helpers rarely fire outside HTTP context).
None of these helpers accept a token secret / PAT plaintext as input. Callers that need to work with tokens use the auth actions directly (RefreshAction, LoginAction, etc.).
